summary: Found material violations in import-tool path/output handling, unvalidated source IDs reaching Mongo filters, and cross-family deterministic ID collision risk.

findings.invariant: (1) READ-ONLY-ON-SOURCE: the import tool must never create, modify, rename, or delete ANYTHING under the source export directory - the journal and every output must land outside it (CWD/target). Any code path that can write into the source dir violates this.
findings.title: Journal path can be inside the source export directory
findings.recommendation: Resolve `--source` and the effective journal path, then reject any journal path contained by the source directory, including the default when `process.cwd()` is the source directory.

findings.invariant: (1) READ-ONLY-ON-SOURCE: the import tool must never create, modify, rename, or delete ANYTHING under the source export directory - the journal and every output must land outside it (CWD/target). Any code path that can write into the source dir violates this.
findings.title: Execute outputs can be directed under the source export directory
findings.recommendation: Resolve `sourceDir` and `contentDataDir` before execution and reject a missing, equal, child, or otherwise source-contained target/output directory before blob or prose writes begin.

findings.invariant: (2) PATH CONTAINMENT: file paths derived from source data (manifest entries, blob path-rewrite, integration_skills subpaths, upload paths) must be contained - a hostile source export containing ../ or absolute paths must not cause reads outside the source root or writes outside the intended target/journal locations.
findings.title: Rewritten artifact paths include unsanitized source IDs
findings.recommendation: Validate or encode `a._id` as a single safe storage segment before embedding it in `screenshotPath`; reject IDs containing path separators, dot segments, or other non-segment characters.

findings.invariant: (4) INJECTION: strings derived from source-export data must never reach a shell/exec/eval; imported JSON must not smuggle MongoDB operators (keys beginning with $ or containing dots, $where etc.) into queries/updates as OPERATORS rather than literal data (NoSQL injection / query-shape corruption on import or verification reads).
findings.title: Unvalidated source `_id` values reach Mongo upsert filters
findings.recommendation: Runtime-validate every imported document before planning/execution: `_id` must be a string safe for literal identity use, and reject keys beginning with `$` or containing dots before any `Store.put` or verification query can observe them.

findings.invariant: (6) IDEMPOTENT-ID SAFETY: the deterministic _id derivation must not allow two DISTINCT source records (e.g. from different orgs/users/collections) to derive the same _id such that one silently overwrites the other on import.
findings.title: Integration-definition IDs can collide with imported integration_config IDs
findings.recommendation: Before execution, check all planned docs per target collection for duplicate `_id`s across families, or use a target collection/ID namespace that cannot collide with source `integration_configs` rows.
